metaTitle: Foot & Ankle Pain Support | Avana Surgical Systems
metaDescription: Plantar fasciitis, ankle sprain, achilles tendonitis, and post-surgical foot support. Premium bracing fitted across India.

hero.title: Walk further. Hurt less.
hero.description: From plantar fasciitis that aches with every step to recovery from an ankle sprain or surgery — clinically proven support for confident movement.
hero.imageAlt: Foot and ankle pain bracing support — landing page hero

conditions.title: Plantar Fasciitis
conditions.description: Sharp heel pain especially with the first steps of the day — supported by night splints and arch support.

conditions.title: Ankle Sprain
conditions.description: Ligament injury from rolling the ankle. Functional bracing protects against re-injury during return to activity.

conditions.title: Achilles Tendonitis
conditions.description: Pain along the back of the heel from overuse — supported by heel lifts and offloading bracing.

conditions.title: Post-Surgical Recovery
conditions.description: After ankle surgery or fracture, controlled-ankle-motion (CAM) walkers protect healing.

seoContent.title: Understanding foot & ankle pain
seoContent.intro: Your feet take every step you make. Pain there changes everything — work, exercise, even sleep.

sections.heading: Why is my heel killing me in the morning?
sections.body: Classic plantar fasciitis. The plantar fascia tightens overnight; the first steps stretch it sharply. Night splints, supportive footwear, and stretching usually resolve it within months.

sections.heading: Should I brace an ankle sprain?
sections.body: Yes — the evidence is strong. Functional bracing during early recovery and return-to-sport reduces re-sprain risk significantly compared to no brace.

seoContent.disclaimer: This page is educational. Please consult an orthopaedic specialist for persistent foot or ankle pain.

faq.question: How long should I wear an ankle brace after a sprain?
faq.answer: Typically 4–6 weeks during all weight-bearing activity, then continued use during sport for at least the first season back to reduce re-sprain risk.

faq.question: Will a night splint help my plantar fasciitis?
faq.answer: For many patients, yes. Night splints keep the plantar fascia gently stretched overnight, reducing morning pain.

cta.title: Get back on your feet — comfortably.
